hard.next_step: Has to build out Business Case wtih internal Champion
hard.ais_why: Procurement lead set a personal AI mandate to increase AI usage this year; leadership team in AI mentoring; explicit pull for agentic sourcing and Merlin insights; commercial intent ('How much does it cost?') plus a free POC offer.
hard.primary_competitor: Do-nothing / status quo (manual Excel + PowerPoint reporting, incumbent Scanmarket)

north_star_verdict.headline: Suvi Lehtonen returned a first-draft business case on 1 Jul (excluding purchasing and paying, with a rough cost estimate), so the champion is actively carrying the deal; the open work is converting that draft into a quantified ROI case and getting the Group CSO to sponsor it before the summer break stalls momentum.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 27 Mar 2026, Qualified since 30 Mar (~3.3 months in stage). Close date pushed 16 Oct to 31 Dec 2026 on 29 May; 178 days out. Discovery 26 Mar, full demo 17 Apr, template sent 18 Jun, buyer-returned business-case draft 1 Jul (5 days ago). Pace is consistent with a 12-15 month enterprise motion; next milestone is the business-case presentation to the Group CSO, no slip required, date credible.
evidence: Buyer-side momentum on 1 Jul 2026: Suvi Lehtonen emailed Casper a first-draft business case, writing 'I did a very quick first draft. Purchasing and paying would be outside of the scope, so did not fill in those parts. At this stage a very rough estimation of the cost' (source: SF Task/Clari email received 1 Jul 2026, LastActivityDate).

Champion engaged and multi-threaded: Suvi (Senior Sourcing Manager, primary) and Ossi Heikkinen (Purchasing Manager) drove discovery 26 Mar and the 1.5-hour demo 17 Apr, and are co-authoring the value case for the Group CSO (source: SF Next_Step__c 'Has to build out Business Case wtih internal Champion'; Avoma demo 17 Apr 2026).

Strong AI appetite: AIS 8/Strong; Ossi confirmed agentic sourcing 'could even work' for both indirect and direct spend and both leads pulled hard on Merlin insights and autonomous sourcing (source: Avoma demo 17 Apr 2026, speaker Ossi Heikkinen).

Forecast Pipeline with $150k ACV understates a live, champion-driven deal with an approved free POC and an in-flight business case; Best Case is defensible, but the CSO economic buyer has not yet been on a call and no ROI number exists, so it is not yet Commit.
Watch: buyer flagged 'busy times' on 1 Jul and the Nordic summer holiday window could delay the CSO presentation; no concrete customer-named date for the presentation yet.

metrics.narrative: The business problem is well established but not yet quantified. On 17 Apr Ossi Heikkinen pressed for one-click, always-current category reporting: 'if I print it out today, it's old already after one hour... just press the button, follow the link, and then you have that' (source: Avoma demo 17 Apr 2026, speaker Ossi Heikkinen), and on 26 Mar the sourcing team described building category reports manually in Excel and PowerPoint with version-control pain. As of 1 Jul the buyer is putting numbers to it herself: Suvi Lehtonen sent 'a very rough estimation of the cost' inside her first-draft business case (source: SF email 1 Jul 2026). No savings, cycle-time, or headcount figure has been agreed yet, so the move is to co-build the ROI model with Suvi (report-cycle time saved, tail-spend automation savings) before it reaches the Group CSO.
sources: Avoma demo 17 Apr 2026, speaker Ossi Heikkinen

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is the Group Chief Sustainability and Sourcing Officer (the CSO the champions report into); the entire next step exists to build a business case to present to that office (source: SF Next_Step__c 'Has to build out Business Case wtih internal Champion'), and the AIS rationale notes 'our whole leadership team is getting AI mentoring', signalling top-down AI sponsorship. The CSO has not personally appeared on either the 26 Mar or 17 Apr call, so authority is inferred from the reporting line and the business-case-to-leadership process, not yet from direct commercial engagement. Budget authority is therefore mapped but not activated: the move is for Suvi to book the business-case presentation and secure the CSO's sponsorship in writing.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria were surfaced concretely across the demo. Ossi Heikkinen made real-time, self-refreshing category dashboards and one-click PowerPoint export a hard requirement ('press the button... and then you have that', 17 Apr), and demanded external market-data integration for direct materials, naming LME aluminium pricing plus their subscriptions Vesper, Expana (formerly Mintec) and Zedex ('we are following the aluminium price in LME... get also a lot of insight on future prices', 17 Apr). Agentic/autonomous sourcing for tail spend was validated by Ossi as viable ('it could even work... especially when it comes to indirect', 17 Apr), and contract clause comparison / side-by-side redlining was an explicit ask he wanted to see. Guided buying via Microsoft Teams (Merlin Intake) landed as a future-phase interest. These are confirmed evaluation criteria, quoted from the buyer on the 17 Apr demo.
sources: Avoma demo 17 Apr 2026, speakers Ossi Heikkinen and Arthur Kaufmanen

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running an internal build-the-case process rather than a formal RFP: discovery 26 Mar, a full solution demo 17 Apr with the category team, then a Zycus business-case template sent 18 Jun which Suvi returned as a first draft on 1 Jul. The path is champion-led, bottom-up: Suvi and Ossi assemble the value case, then present to the Group CSO for budget approval after summer. There is no committee vote, no procurement/legal gate opened yet, and no customer-named decision date. The approval chain (who signs beyond the CSO, whether board or finance sign-off is needed) is not yet confirmed, which is the gap to close as the case firms up.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not yet in scope and this is early (Qualified). On 17 Apr the buyer confirmed no legal contract tool today ('I think we don't currently have a tool... our legal is looking into some kind of AI-aided tool', Suvi) and that redlining is manual; they do run DocuSign for e-signature ('we have DocuSign... it is a separate contract linked to our current system'). No MSA, SOW, Order Form, InfoSec review, or partner/SI involvement has been discussed, and Current_Contract_Expiration__c is not populated, so there is no contractual forcing function. Visibility will come once the CSO approves the business case and commercials begin; that is the natural point to scope the paper process.
sources: Avoma demo 17 Apr 2026, speakers Suvi Lehtonen and Ossi Heikkinen

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed and owned by the global sourcing team. Operational: manual, stale reporting, with Ossi stating on 17 Apr that a printed report 'is old already after one hour' and wanting a single button to refresh dashboards and generate PowerPoint decks. Strategic: no real-time market intelligence for direct-materials category strategy, with Ossi wanting LME and forecast feeds pulled into category presentations ('we have to understand... what it might be on tomorrow or after one year', 17 Apr). Compelling event: Suvi's personal target to increase AI usage in procurement this year plus a leadership AI-mentoring programme (SF AIS_Why__c). The pain owners are Suvi Lehtonen and Ossi Heikkinen; the risk if unaddressed is they keep managing categories in Excel and defer any purchase.
sources: Avoma demo 17 Apr 2026, speaker Ossi Heikkinen

champion.narrative: Two active champions. Suvi Lehtonen (Senior Sourcing Manager, primary contact role) initiated the evaluation at a Zycus event, is developing the procurement processes, and is authoring the business case, evidenced by her returning a first draft unprompted on 1 Jul 2026 ('I did a very quick first draft... a very rough estimation of the cost'). Ossi Heikkinen (Purchasing Manager) drove the technical agenda on the 17 Apr demo, championed agentic sourcing for both indirect and direct spend, and set the market-data integration bar. Both have direct access to the Group CSO economic buyer (they report into that office and the case is being built for it). Engagement is current: last buyer touch 1 Jul, 5 days ago. Strength is strong; the only missing piece is converting their draft into a CSO-ready quantified case, not the advocacy itself.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Suvi Lehtonen primary; Ossi Heikkinen Evaluator)

competition.narrative: This is effectively a sole-source, champion-led evaluation with no rival vendor named on either the 26 Mar or 17 Apr calls or in Competitors__c. The real alternative is the status quo: manual Excel/PowerPoint reporting plus an incumbent e-sourcing tool (Scanmarket, used for roughly the critical-supplier ~10% of the base per prior record), both of which the team described as capability gaps rather than preferred options. The wedge is Zycus AI: agentic tail-spend sourcing, Merlin real-time dashboards, and native LME/Vesper/Zedex market-data integration the buyer already subscribes to. The live risk is not a competitor but inertia and the chance that a formal RFP inviting Coupa, SAP Ariba, Ivalua or GEP opens if the business case stalls before CSO sign-off; sole-source status should be confirmed with Suvi before the CSO presentation.

deal_movement.summary: Champion-led momentum is strengthening: after the 18 Jun template send, Suvi returned a first-draft business case on 1 Jul, the most concrete buyer-side action yet, though it explicitly excludes purchasing and paying and carries only a rough cost estimate.

competitive_position.summary: No competing vendor is named in any source (Competitors__c blank; none on the 26 Mar or 17 Apr calls); this is a sole-source, champion-driven evaluation. The single strongest current threat is do-nothing / status quo: Olvi keeps managing categories manually in Excel/PowerPoint alongside the incumbent Scanmarket e-sourcing tool (critical-supplier ~10% of base, described as a capability gap, not a preferred option). Both are dormant threats. The named downstream risk is a formal RFP pulling in Coupa/Ariba/Ivalua/GEP if the business case stalls before the Group CSO signs off.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit on the buyer's stated criteria: real-time dashboards, agentic tail-spend sourcing, market-data integration, and AI-assisted contracting all landed on the 17 Apr demo. The main exposure is proving quantified ROI and confirming the required external-data integrations (Vesper/Expana/Zedex) work on Olvi's own subscriptions.

best_practice_check.summary: Champion-led, sole-source, AI-hungry deal; the win levers are quantifying the case, activating the CSO economic buyer, and converting the offered POC into proof before any RFP opens.
flags: Turn Suvi's first-draft business case into a CSO-grade ROI model (report-cycle time saved, tail-spend savings) before it reaches leadership; no number exists yet.
Get the Group CSO into the room: the economic buyer has never joined a call, so secure Suvi's intro before the business-case presentation.
Convert the offered free POC into a scheduled hands-on trial on Olvi's direct-materials categories to prove agentic sourcing and pre-empt a competitive RFP.
